items.reason: site/index.html and site/docs/index.html render as styled HTML with navigation. No href references to .md/.yaml/.yml under site/ (deterministic check confirms). OpenAPI is rendered via Redoc pages (site/openapi/hosted-json.html, oneroster-core.html, qti-core.html).

site/index.html contains distinct 'Platform Provides' and 'App Developer Owns' sections with concrete bullets naming OneRoster, CASE, QTI, Caliper, LTI, and Open Badges/CLR, plus app-owned items like pedagogy, UI quality, and scope minimization.

Each spec has dictionary/*.v1.json paired with scripts/generate_*.py (e.g., generate_oneroster_core.py emits schema/generated/*.sql, openapi/generated/*.json, docs/generated/*.md, and site/docs/*.html in one run). The generators read the dictionary file and produce all three surfaces; check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies parity.

docs/lead-spec-accounting.md enumerates OneRoster, CASE, QTI, Caliper, LTI 1.3/Advantage, Security Framework, and Data Privacy with corresponding dictionary files. Each dictionary has multiple objects with multiple fields (e.g., integration-governance-core.v1.json covers LTI registration, deployments, launches, services, OAuth clients, scope policies, privacy rules, consent, retention, audit).

Per-field `sourceStandard` references are not present on dictionary fields (only a top-level `source_standard` per file), and the backlog explicitly tracks this as item 10: 'Add explicit per-field `sourceStandard` references and field-level unsupported/deferred ledgers.' Where deferrals are documented (e.g., lead-spec-accounting.md), reasons cite business value rather than 'not yet documented,' but coverage of full OneRoster 1.2, QTI delivery, full LTI services, etc. remains incomplete.

scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py validates object/field/value coverage across all five generated dictionaries. Sampled descriptions are plain language: e.g., person.display_name 'The name shown in user interfaces', class.title 'The class name people see', enrollment.role 'The person's job in the class' — all readable by a non-engineer.

demo/server.js + demo/test.js + demo/schema.sql provide a runnable OneRoster slice. test.js asserts /users/USER-ADA returns sourcedId='USER-ADA' (OneRoster 1.2 shape) and that lookups by internal id correctly 404. Test ends with 'demo-api-ok' and runs in VERIFY.md (`cd demo && npm run reset-db && npm test`).

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py issues live HTTP requests to /rest/v1/people, /class_roster, /gradebook_results with the publishable key against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md confirms verification on 2026-04-26 with 'ok: true' through PostgREST (not static JSON).

site/index.html embeds a sql.js browser console that loads demo/schema.sql + demo/seed.sql and runs read-only queries against real seed data, plus copy-pasteable curl commands against the live Supabase REST URL and Edge Functions. Both execute against real data, not screenshots.

docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md contains 10 decisions covering all required areas (Person identity, Class/course/section, Roles, Enrollment vs. membership, Results/scores, Standards alignment, Identifiers, Time/session, Content/resource, Tenancy). Each entry has prose for specs involved, conflict, choice, mappings table back to each spec, and tradeoff (e.g., DEC-005 explicitly names OneRoster Result, LTI AGS Score/Result, QTI outcomes, Caliper GradeEvent and the projection per spec).

docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md contains a machine-readable JSON block (decision-trace:start markers) listing each `decision_id` with its `produces_fields` array referencing dictionary files like 'dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.display_name'. Each dictionary field also has a `decision_id` attribute (e.g., person.display_name carries 'DEC-001-person-agent-subject'). check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces bidirectional consistency.

supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql adds `tenant_id uuid not null` to every fact table, calls `enable row level security` AND `force row level security`, and creates policies whose USING clauses reference `auth.jwt() ->> 'tenant_id'` (not `true`). pg_policies.snapshot.json confirms forceRowSecurity=true and tenant-claim predicates. tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py creates two distinct Auth users with different tenant_id app_metadata claims and verifies cross-tenant reads are blocked through the live Supabase REST URL.

dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json defines `oauth_scope_policies` mapping each scope (e.g., 'platform.roster.users.directory:read') to exact dictionary field references with privacy_ceiling. Generated OpenAPI (openapi/generated/integration-governance-core.v0.json) emits `platformOAuth` security scheme and per-field `x-oauthScopes`. Every dictionary field carries a `privacy_class`. The scope shape (`platform.roster.users.directory:read` mapped to fields with privacy_class:directory) matches the rubric's 'pass' example.

Every field across the five dictionaries carries a non-empty `privacy_class` from the documented vocabulary (operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, system, public, depends_on_entity, depends_on_contents, privacy_governance). check_dictionary_artifacts.py enforces required field keys including privacy_class. No 'unknown' or empty values observed.

docs/admin-operations.md lists every service-role caller: tests/supabase_tenant_rls_test.py and tests/supabase_audit_log_test.py, each with caller, operation ID, why_user_jwt_insufficient explained substantively (Auth user setup for cross-tenant tests; cannot use user JWT to mint different tenant claims), and last_reviewed=2026-04-26 (within 30 days of research date 2026-04-24). Both test files contain inline comments referencing the matching operation IDs ('# Admin operation: tenant_rls_test_auth_fixture_setup'). No service-role usage detected in supabase/functions/* or demo/*.

supabase/migrations/0001 creates `audit_log` table with required columns (client_id, scope, purpose, field_accessed, tenant_id, timestamp, plus subject_id, request_path, etc.) and a `read_people_sensitive_audited` SECURITY DEFINER function that inserts one audit row per restricted field (display_name, given_name, family_name, email, primary_role) before returning. supabase/functions/audited-roster-read forwards user JWT and calls the RPC. tests/supabase_audit_log_test.py verifies live audit rows are written with all required fields and that direct REST reads of restricted columns are blocked.

docs/spec-gap-backlog.md contains a 'Rubric Trace For Open Items' table that maps rubric IDs to backlog entries (lead_spec_full_accounting, tenant_isolation_enforced, oauth_scopes_mapped_to_fields, audit_log_for_sensitive_reads, edge_functions_*, rls_enabled_on_referenced_tables). The lone partial item (lead_spec_full_accounting) is tracked as backlog item 10 with status 'Partial' and substantive owner notes.

docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md has columns for 'Structured source', 'Generated artifacts', 'Runnable slice', and 'Unsupported ledger' populated honestly per spec (e.g., QTI shows 'No' under Runnable slice; OneRoster core shows 'Yes'; Caliper shows 'Partial: authenticated Edge Function receipt path').

supabase/functions/ contains gradebook-bulk-submit, lti-launch-handler, caliper-event-ingestion, oauth-token-exchange, and audited-roster-read. Each has a deno.json and index.ts. All construct the Supabase client from `req.headers.get('Authorization')` so RLS applies as the calling user; none use SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. site/index.html and docs/supabase-hosted-database.md document the live function URLs with working curl examples.

Every Edge Function reads the Authorization header (e.g., 'const authorization = req.headers.get("Authorization")') and passes it as `global.headers.Authorization` when constructing the Supabase client. None reference SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY; all use SUPABASE_ANON_KEY/SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY. docs/admin-operations.md confirms no Edge Function callers are on the service-role allowlist.

supabase/policies/pg_policies.snapshot.json shows every public table (organizations, people, classes, courses, enrollments, line_items, results, source_identifiers, academic_sessions, audit_log) with rowsecurity=true, forceRowSecurity=true, and at least one policy whose USING clause is a non-trivial expression referencing `auth.jwt() ->> 'tenant_id'` and table columns. None use `using (true)`. generatedAt=2026-04-26T11:07:15Z, consistent with recent schema commits.

scripts/check_supabase_rest.py exercises /people, /class_roster, /gradebook_results against the live Supabase URL and is invoked as part of the documented reviewer path. docs/supabase-hosted-database.md confirms the schema, seed, smoke checks, and REST verification succeeded on 2026-04-26 against PostgREST (not a static mirror).

site/index.html includes a copy-pasteable curl with the publishable key against https://qzxlgrerjoiamxvnkklq.supabase.co/rest/v1/people. The publishable key is also documented in .env.example and docs/supabase-hosted-database.md. No private credentials are required.

VERIFY.md's last command is `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`. scripts/evaluate_platform.py emits a per-rubric-item array with status/reason/evidence plus an overall counts summary (pass/partial/fail/blocked/total) and a `done` flag.

scripts/check_spec_conformance.py still runs in VERIFY.md and writes site/api/spec-conformance.json with `notACompletionClaim: true` and explicit advisory-only scope. scripts/codex_loop.py's maybe_publish gates on the LLM evaluator's rubric_progressed/done state, not the deterministic score; the evaluator builds its prompt with the deterministic report included as one signal (load_advisory_report in evaluate_platform.py).

scripts/codex_loop.py defines `read_overrides()`, `overrides_paused()` (matches lone PAUSE token outside HTML comments), and `format_overrides_for_prompt()`. The main loop calls `overrides_paused(overrides)` BEFORE invoking Codex and short-circuits with 'Halting before iteration' if set. build_prompt() injects the formatted overrides into a 'Human overrides' labeled section. site/docs/index.html shows loop-overrides.html is rendered, confirming docs/loop-overrides.md exists.

scripts/codex_loop.py terminates on `after_eval.get('done')` from the LLM evaluator (not a numeric score) and applies hard caps via `max_iterations`, `stall_limit` (consecutive iterations without rubric progress), and documented cost-ceiling rail. The evaluator's `done` is true only when fail+partial=0 and any blocked items have prerequisites.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.
